subject: Language Arts
title: Grade 1 Language Arts
subtitle: Building Readers and Writers Through Story, Sound, and Conversation

units.title: Sounds and Letters
units.description: Students develop phonemic awareness and phonics skills by exploring letter-sound relationships, blending sounds into words, and breaking words into their component sounds. The unit builds the decoding foundation all future reading depends on.

lessons.title: Letters Have Sounds
lessons.big_idea: Every letter makes at least one sound, and knowing those sounds helps you read any word.
key_concepts: vowels say their name and a short sound

lessons.title: Blending Sounds Into Words
lessons.big_idea: When you push sounds together smoothly, you can read words you have never seen before.

lessons.title: Breaking Words Into Sounds
lessons.big_idea: Segmenting a word into its individual sounds helps you spell it and understand how it works.

lessons.title: Tricky Sounds: Digraphs and Blends
lessons.big_idea: Some letter pairs make one new sound — knowing them unlocks hundreds of new words.
key_concepts: consonant digraphs: sh, ch, th, wh
consonant blends: bl, cr, st, tr
reading and spelling words with digraphs and blends

lessons.title: Unit 1 Review: Sounds and Letters All Around Us
lessons.big_idea: The sounds we know and the letters that spell them are the building blocks of every word we will ever read.

units.title: Reading Stories
units.description: Students read and listen to literary texts, learning to identify characters, settings, and key events while making connections between stories and their own lives. They practice retelling as a comprehension strategy.

lessons.title: What Makes a Story
lessons.big_idea: Every story has characters who want something, a setting where it happens, and a problem to solve.
key_concepts: story structure as a predictable pattern

lessons.title: Getting to Know Characters
lessons.big_idea: A character's words, actions, and feelings tell you who they really are.
key_concepts: using text evidence to describe a character
how characters change across a story

lessons.title: Retelling a Story
lessons.big_idea: Retelling uses the story's own order — beginning, middle, and end — to show you understood what happened.

lessons.title: Comparing Two Stories
lessons.big_idea: When you compare two stories about similar topics, you notice what makes each one special.
key_concepts: using a simple Venn diagram or T-chart

lessons.title: Asking and Answering Questions
lessons.big_idea: Asking who, what, where, when, why, and how keeps your brain active while you read.
key_concepts: question words: who, what, where, when, why, how
generating your own questions before and while reading

lessons.title: Unit 2 Review: Story Detectives
lessons.big_idea: Good readers notice characters, settings, key events, and story structure — and they ask questions as they go.
key_concepts: story structure: character, setting, problem, solution

units.title: Reading to Learn
units.description: Students explore informational texts, learning to find the main topic, identify key details, and use text features like headers, captions, and bold words to locate information. They practice explaining what they learned in their own words.

lessons.title: What Is Informational Text?
lessons.big_idea: Informational books teach facts about the real world, and they use special features to help you find information fast.
key_concepts: text features: title, heading, caption, bold word, table of contents
author's purpose in nonfiction: to inform

lessons.title: Main Topic and Key Details
lessons.big_idea: The main topic is what the whole text is about — every key detail adds one more fact about that topic.
key_concepts: supporting details strengthen the main idea

lessons.title: Connecting Words and Pictures in Nonfiction
lessons.big_idea: In informational books, images and diagrams add details the words alone do not tell you.
key_concepts: information from images vs. information from text

lessons.title: Unit 3 Review: Nonfiction Explorers
lessons.big_idea: Readers of informational texts use text features, main topic, key details, and images together to understand and remember what they learned.
key_concepts: main topic and supporting key details
images and diagrams as information sources

units.title: Writing to Share Ideas
units.description: Students develop as writers by learning to plan, draft, and share three types of writing: opinion, informational, and narrative. They practice using spaces between words, capitals, and end punctuation.

lessons.title: Telling What You Think: Opinion Writing
lessons.big_idea: An opinion tells what you believe, and a reason tells why someone else should agree with you.

lessons.title: Teaching With Words: Informational Writing
lessons.big_idea: An informational piece names a topic and uses facts and definitions to teach the reader something real.

lessons.title: Telling a Story: Narrative Writing
lessons.big_idea: A narrative uses details and a clear sequence of events to put your reader right inside the story.
key_concepts: using temporal words: first, next, then, finally
adding details to help the reader see and feel

lessons.title: Writing Sentences the Right Way
lessons.big_idea: Every sentence begins with a capital letter, ends with a punctuation mark, and leaves spaces between words — these rules help your reader understand you.
key_concepts: capitalization at sentence start and for proper nouns
end punctuation: period, question mark, exclamation point
finger spaces between words in draft writing

lessons.title: Revising and Sharing Our Writing
lessons.big_idea: Writers read their own work out loud to find places where they can add details, fix mistakes, or make sentences clearer.
key_concepts: adding a detail to make writing stronger

lessons.title: Digital Writing: Using Technology to Publish
lessons.big_idea: Typing and publishing your writing on a screen is another way to share your ideas with the world.
key_concepts: digital tools for writing and publishing
sharing writing digitally with teacher guidance

lessons.title: Unit 4 Review: Writer's Showcase
lessons.big_idea: Strong writers use the right structure for each type of writing, follow sentence rules, and revise to make their message clear.
key_concepts: three types of writing: opinion, informational, narrative
